steps.title: Identify what the data must show
steps.content: The assertion predicts that cooler months correlate with lower reproductive fitness. Wing centroid size proxies for body size, which correlates with fitness, so we need data showing that a cooler month had smaller wings than a warmer month.

steps.title: Match to the supporting data
steps.content:
Choice D demonstrates this: female wing centroid size was smaller in September (a cooler month) than in July (a warmer month), supporting the predicted relationship between temperature and body size/fitness. Choices B and C don't compare seasonal wing variation (B shows sex differences within months, C provides temperature alone), and choice A does not match the table data.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
